sutta: monks there are these 8 rebirths due to making gifts 1 day here in mounts a man makes a gift to a recruit or brahmana in the shape of food drink clothes vehicle flowers sand ointment bedding dwelling and lights he gives hoping for a return he sees wealthy nobles brahmanas and householders surrounded by attended by enjoying the 5 strands of sensual pleasure and he thinks ah if only i can be reborn among them on the breaking up of the body after death and he fixes his mind on this thought directs his attention to it and makes the thought become his mind set on low things is not made to become for the beyond and he is reborn after death among the wealthy men and i say this of the virtuous not of the vicious monks the mental aspiration of the virtuous prospers because of its purity then again monks a man making a similar gift and expecting a return hearing that the company of the 4 royal devas are long-lived beautiful and very happy might wish to be reborn among them all among the devas of the 33 tabatimsa heaven or among the yama devas or among the tusita devas or among the devas who delight in creating or among the devas who have power over others creations he fixes his mind on this thought directs his attention to it and makes the thought become his mind set on low things is not made to become for the beyond and he is reborn after death among those gave us and i say this of the virtues not of the vicious monks the mental aspiration of the virtuous prospers because of its purity all hearing that the devas of brahma's retinue are long-lived beautiful and very happy he might wish to be reborn among them and he fixes his mind thereon and is reborn after death among those devas and i say this of the virtues not of the wishes of him who is rid of lust not of him who lusts for the mental aspiration of the virtuous monks prospers because of its lustlessness monks these are the 8 rebirths due to making gifts that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
so here the buddha is saying that a person a virtuous person makes offerings to uh recluses and brahmins and then he keeps hoping to get something in return and he makes an aspiration to be born as a wealthy man or as a deva and he because he's virtuous he his aspiration succeeds and he's born among them but this the buddha said his mind is set on low things that means all things means he he wants to be reborn so he does not [Music] um you know he does not go beyond beyond means out of this world uh whereas if a person makes a gift without any selfish thought now of getting something is in return then he practices selfless selflessly then there is a chance for him to go to the beyond because the beyond is selfless so it is not wise when we make an offering when we make do charity to think of getting something in return


===== SOURCE: 075_Anguttara Nikaya Book 8F 8436 - 8549 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.txt =====
